details: @catamania/front-components@1.0.2 poses as a UI component library but its postinstall.js collects host reconnaissance data (hostname, username, and current working directory via os.hostname()/os.userInfo()/process.cwd()) and POSTs the beacon to hxxps://webhook[.]site/9bbf9333-af82-4597-90d8-7da9162ed500. Environment-variable names are gathered with values masked. A single trivial Vue component is window-dressing; the postinstall's sole purpose is silent host-metadata exfiltration, consistent with first-stage triage ahead of a later payload. @catamania/front-components@1.0.2 is disguised as a UI components library with a trivial decoy Vue component. Its postinstall hook collects hostname, username, current working directory and environment-variable keys and POSTs them to hxxps://webhook[.]site/9bbf9333-af82-4597-90d8-7da9162ed500. There is no README or repository URL. This is a host-metadata reconnaissance beacon aimed at identifying high-value targets.
